steps.title: Coalesce and launch the Experiential Learning Community
steps.summary: Build on work already under way through the July-August and September-October planning windows to bring together the founding experiential learning community, clarify its initial priorities and deliver the confirmed September 2026 launch under interim institutional leadership.

outputs.title: Experiential Learning Community launch
outputs.summary: A September launch with a founding membership, initial priorities and a clear rhythm for continued participation.

steps.title: Develop the governance, resourcing and faculty support model
steps.summary: Develop the operating model from July through October 2026, including the remit of the Innovation Committee, interim and funded convenorship, community support, funded projects, accountability and the indicative resource envelope. Work with faculties to agree the principles through which future community convenors can receive release, workload allocation or backfill support, including central and faculty contributions, eligibility, duration and review.

outputs.title: Governance and resourcing model
outputs.summary: An agreed model ready to support the establishment of the Innovation Committee and the development of the communities.

outputs.title: Agreed release and backfill principles
outputs.summary: A sufficiently clear and equitable institutional offer to support the funded convenor call from January 2027.

steps.title: Establish the Innovation Committee
steps.summary: Stand up the Innovation Committee in the November-December planning window with an agreed remit, membership and authority to govern the communities, convenorship and funded activity within the developing model.

outputs.title: Operational Innovation Committee
outputs.summary: A governing committee able to steward the communities and the transition to funded leadership and activity.

steps.title: Confirm interim convenors and integrate the remaining communities
steps.summary: In the November-December planning window, identify existing institutional leaders to act as interim, unremitted convenors and use their leadership to bring the AI in Education and Inclusive Education communities into the shared institutional frame by December 2026.

outputs.title: Three communities operating in a common frame
outputs.summary: Experiential Learning, AI in Education and Inclusive Education each have interim leadership, a founding constituency and an initial programme of activity.

steps.title: Run funded convenor and community project calls
steps.summary: Launch coordinated calls for funded Community Convenors and community-aligned projects in January-February 2027, then complete selection, confirm faculty release and backfill arrangements, appoint convenors and agree the first funded project portfolio in March-April.

outputs.title: Open institutional calls
outputs.summary: Published calls with clear eligibility, support, selection criteria, expectations and decision routes.

outputs.title: Funded leadership and project portfolio
outputs.summary: Appointed convenors and an approved first set of projects that strengthen the communities and their shared work.

steps.title: Build the first three communities and establish their operating conventions
steps.summary: From January to August 2027, develop the Experiential Learning, AI in Education and Inclusive Education communities into active and recognisable communities of practice. Through their early work, establish and test the shared conventions for convenorship, participation, communication, collaborative inquiry, student and partner involvement, funded activity, decision-making and routes into institutional influence. This will create three functioning communities and a clear basis for how future strategic communities of practice can be established and supported at King's.

outputs.title: Three established communities with shared operating conventions
outputs.summary: Three communities with supported leadership, sustained participation and credible programmes of activity, alongside an agreed and tested set of conventions to guide future communities of practice.

steps.title: Review the first three communities and agree the expansion portfolio
steps.summary: From September to December 2027, produce the first impact report and review participation, activity, governance, convenorship, faculty release and backfill, funding, project mechanisms, shared operating conventions and emerging institutional influence. Use the findings to refine the model and approve three additional strategic communities for the 2028-29 academic year.

outputs.title: First impact and operating-model review
outputs.summary: An evidence-led assessment of the first three communities, with agreed refinements to governance, resourcing and operating arrangements.

outputs.title: Approved six-community portfolio
outputs.summary: Three additional strategic communities agreed for mobilisation and operation in the 2028-29 academic year.

steps.title: Confirm convenors, resources and launch the 2028-29 project call
steps.summary: In January-February 2028, confirm or recruit convenors for all six communities and secure the release, workload allocation, backfill and operational support required for September 2028-August 2029. At the same time, agree the community and project funding envelope, translate the review priorities into a coordinated institutional call, confirm eligibility and assessment arrangements, and launch the 2028-29 call for community-aligned projects by February.

outputs.title: Confirmed convenors and resource arrangements
outputs.summary: Leadership, faculty release or backfill and operational support agreed in time for the 2028-29 workload cycle.

outputs.title: Open 2028-29 project call
outputs.summary: An institutional call for projects aligned to the approved six-community portfolio, supported by an agreed funding envelope and selection process.

steps.title: Select projects and mobilise the expanded portfolio
steps.summary: From March to August 2028, assess and select community-aligned projects, confirm project leads, funding and delivery arrangements, and establish the three additional communities. Build their founding memberships, priorities and first-year plans, and prepare all six communities, convenors and funded projects to operate through a common framework from September 2028.

outputs.title: Approved 2028-29 funded project portfolio
outputs.summary: Selected projects with confirmed leads, funding, expectations and routes into the work of the six communities.

outputs.title: Expanded portfolio ready to operate
outputs.summary: Three additional communities established and all six communities, convenors and projects mobilised for September 2028.

steps.title: Operate the expanded six-community portfolio
steps.summary: Operate all six strategic communities through the September 2028-August 2029 academic year, supporting their programmes of activity, funded projects, institutional influence and cross-community learning while gathering consistent evidence of participation, outcomes and resource requirements.

outputs.title: Full academic year of six-community operation
outputs.summary: A complete cycle of activity and evidence from the expanded portfolio, including the contribution of its funded projects.

steps.title: Complete the second impact and operating-model review
steps.summary: From September to December 2029, review the six-community portfolio after a complete academic year of operation. Assess impact and sustainability, determine which communities should continue and whether the portfolio should evolve, and agree recommendations for recurrent resources, permanent governance and ownership, and the annual process for review, renewal and commissioning.

outputs.title: Second impact report
outputs.summary: An assessment of the impact, effectiveness and sustainability of the six-community portfolio after a full academic-year cycle.

outputs.title: Recommended permanent institutional model
outputs.summary: Agreed recommendations for the continuing portfolio, recurrent resources, governance, ownership and annual commissioning rhythm.

steps.title: Confirm the permanent BAU model and resource settlement
steps.summary: In January-February 2030, approve the continuing community portfolio and confirm convenors, release, backfill, central support and project funding for 2030-31. Agree the permanent institutional owner, governance route, budget holder, recurrent resource settlement and annual planning cycle in time for the following academic year's workload and budget decisions.

outputs.title: Approved BAU model and resource settlement
outputs.summary: A permanently owned, governed and recurrently resourced model, with the 2030-31 portfolio and leadership arrangements confirmed.

steps.title: Transition to business as usual
steps.summary: From March to September 2030, transfer governance, budgets, reporting, operational support and accountability from mobilisation to the permanent institutional owner. Complete the necessary handovers so that the Strategic Communities of Practice operate as an institutionally owned, recurrent programme from the start of the 2030-31 academic year.

outputs.title: Business-as-usual transition complete
outputs.summary: Strategic Communities of Practice operating through permanent ownership, governance, resources and annual planning arrangements from September 2030.
